Human Resource Executive Job at Garment Care Limited




        
    

        
        Garment Care Limited is a professional garment care company specializing in dry cleaning, laundry, garment finishing, and fabric care services. The company is committed to delivering high-quality cleaning solutions, exceptional customer service, and reliable care for both everyday clothing and specialty garments, using modern techniques and quality standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Human Resource Executive

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	The HR Executive supports the daily operations of the Human Resources department by managing recruitment, employee records, onboarding, performance management, employee relations, and ensuring compliance with company policies and labor regulations.


Responsibilities


	Coordinate recruitment, selection, and onboarding activities.
	Maintain accurate employee records and HR documentation.
	Administer leave, attendance, and payroll-related processes.
	Support employee engagement and performance management initiatives.
	Ensure compliance with HR policies and employment laws.
	Handle employee inquiries and provide HR support.
	Prepare HR reports and maintain confidentiality of employee information.


Education


	Bachelor's Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Psychology, or a related field.
	Professional HR certification is an added advantage.


Experience:


	2 - 4 years of relevant experience in Human Resources.


Skills:


	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Excellent organizational and time management abilities.
	Attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and HR software.
	Ability to maintain confidentiality and  work effectively in a team.


Other Requirements:


	Strong understanding of labor laws and HR best practices.
	High level of integrity and professionalism.
	Ability to multitask and work under pressure.
	Willingness to learn and adapt in a fast-paced work environment.


Benefits


	Competitive salary - N150,000 - N200,000 monthly.
	Staff bus transportation.
	Free daily lunch.
	Health Maintenance Organization (HMO) coverage.
	Pension contributions in line with statutory regulations.
	Paid annual leave and public holidays.
	Opportunities for learning, training, and career growth.
	Supportive and collaborative work environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a cover letter to: [email protected] using "HR Executive" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

  "description": "Search for Common Ground (Search) is an international non-profit organisation that promotes the peaceful transformation of conflict. With headquarters in Washington, DC, and Brussels, Belgium, Search&rsquo;s mission is to transform how individuals, organisations, and governments deal with conflict, moving away from destructive approaches and towards cooperative solutions. With more than 700 staff worldwide, Search implements projects in more than 30 countries in Africa, Asia, Europe, the Middle East, North Africa, and North America. Search for Common Ground has fully integrated the Lake Chad Basin Programme, representing a strategic, multi-dimensional, and coordinated conflict transformation initiative across Nigeria, Cameroon, and Chad.

The Nigeria Office is part of Search&rsquo;s Lake Chad Basin Country Programme which leverages a mature, nationwide operational platform in Nigeria established in 2004, a strategically expanding mission in Cameroon officially registered in August 2023, and a purposefully re-established presence in Chad returned in 2023 following an initial operational phase from 2011 to 2017. Fundamentally grounded in rigorous, locally validated conflict analysis and deploying our signature Common Ground methodology, the programme systematically empowers women and youth as central agents of peace, strengthens indigenous conflict resolution mechanisms, and mainstreams peacebuilding objectives into humanitarian and development frameworks through the Humanitarian-Development-Peace Nexus approach

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Procurement, Admin &amp; Logistics Assistant
Job Locations: Benue and Nasarawa
Employment Type: Full-time
Duration: 17 months

Background of the Project


	The project aims to improve peace and security in Nigerian communities within Benue and Nasarawa. This will be achieved through community capacity building, institutional strengthening, and cross-border collaboration.
	The project start-up phase includes a baseline study, community entry and advocacy, security assessments, and conflict scans to inform targeted interventions.
	Activities focus on empowering community members through training in conflict sensitivity and early warning systems, establishing and revitalizing local government level and state-level Peace Architecture Dialogues, and fostering collaboration amongst project stakeholders.
	The project also seeks to promote trust-building and social cohesion, through approaches such as sport activities between communities and security officials, media engagement for conflict-sensitive journalism, and community outreach.
	By fostering inclusive dialogue and enhancing security coordination, the project aims to improve perceptions of peace and security while building sustainable mechanisms for conflict resolution and prevention in the States.


Position Summary


	The Procurement, Administration and Logistics (PAL) Assistant will provide the logistical support to the Lafia and Makurdi office, working closely with the PAL Officer under the Coordination of the Senior PAL Officer and the finance team to ensure good recordkeeping, as well as liaising with the housekeeping staff to support the smooth running of the office.
	S/he will&nbsp;work under the direct supervision of the PAL Senior Officer and closely with the finance and programs team. The position will support the supervision of the office cleaning staff and project driver in their office. The position will be based in Makurdi, Benue State and Lafia, Nassarawa State with field travels to the relevant communities


Major duties and responsibilities


	Liaise with the landlord of the office premises and other operational contacts such as vendors, suppliers and courier services to keep the office fully functional
	Coordinate package delivery and pick up
	Ensure that Search&rsquo;s inventory list is updated on regular intervals
	Develop and oversee safety protocols, including the management of the security company
	Ensure that the door security system and key management is functioning properly
	Manage the telephone/intercom/internet system
	Provide logistical support for meetings and conferences on-site and off-site
	Respond to general emails and forward to the appropriate members of staff
	Provide orientation for new staff, visitors or consultants on the use of the general office equipment and other office systems
	Maintain office equipment and oversee repairs
	Manage the office fleet and approve any private vehicle hire
	Oversee vehicle repairs
	Maintain office logs on travel, inventory and fuel usage for generators, vehicles etc.
	Prepare travel arrangements, relevant documentation (tickets, visas etc.) and any necessary travel materials (Meeting confirmations, background or reference confirmations)
	Prepare and submit a monthly logistics report about the above to the Procurement, Admin and Logistics Senior Officer.
	Coordinate procurement process of goods and services, ensure necessary documentation using the Global Procurement System (GPS)
	Support to maintain and update vendor&rsquo;s database for necessary goods and services
	Monitor quality and control cost on various office supplies, stationery, hotel accommodation, transportation and other equipment
	Provide support to other teams where needed
	Any other function required to undertake broadly in line with above functions.
	As job descriptions cannot be exhaustive, the position holder may be required to undertake other duties that are broadly in line with the above key duties.


Qualifications
Education:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Management, procurement, Accounting or other social sciences.


Required Experience:


	1 - 3 years of professional experience providing finance, administrative, logistical and/or operational support
	Experience working in an NGO or civil society is a plus
	Experience working with little or minimal supervision


Other Relevant Requirements:


	Resident in Lafia or Makurdi
	Proven interest &amp; commitment to humanitarian and development principles and a demonstrable understanding of conflict/post conflict development contexts
	Excellent in English (oral and written)
	Proven ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines; team player and effective communicator,
	Strong IT skills (Email, Microsoft Office applications, Google Drive, Google Docs, Sheets, Presentation).
	Good reporting capacity and experience
	Ability to function effectively under stressful conditions such as heavy workloads, and deadlines.


Note


	Applicants should submit their Current resume and Cover Letter (which includes expectations of compensation and projected start date).

Personal Banking Officer (PBO) Job at Duxbank




        
    

        
        Duxbank is a future-focused Nigerian financial institution designed to reshape how young people experience money. Positioned at the intersection of fintech and lifestyle, Duxbank is building a digital-first banking platform that prioritizes financial inclusion, innovation, and simplicity.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Personal Banking Officer (PBO)

Job Location: Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	A Personal Banking Officer is responsible for acquiring, managing, and growing customer relationships while promoting the bank’s products and services to meet deposit, loan, and profitability targets.


Essential Functions / Key Responsibilities
Customer Acquisition & Relationship Management:


	Source and onboard new individual and business customers.
	Build, nurture, and maintain strong relationships with existing account holders.
	Conduct regular customer site visits and ongoing follow-ups.


Deposit Mobilization:


	Drive growth across savings, current, and fixed deposit accounts.
	Consistently meet or exceed assigned deposit mobilization targets.
	Formulate and execute strategies to attract low-cost deposits.


Loan Generation & Portfolio Monitoring:


	Identify potential borrowers and market the bank's credit/loan products.
	Perform preliminary credit assessments and financial evaluations.
	Monitor loan repayments to preserve portfolio quality.
	Actively follow up on overdue loans and support debt recovery efforts.


Cross-Selling &  Business Development:


	Cross-sell digital banking products, fixed deposits, and other financial services to increase customer product penetration.
	Identify new market opportunities, generate quality leads, and participate in community and market outreach events.


Customer Service & Operational Compliance:


	Address customer inquiries and resolve complaints promptly to ensure high satisfaction.
	Strictly adhere to KYC (Know Your Customer), AML (Anti-Money Laundering), and regulatory requirements.
	Maintain complete documentation for account opening and loan applications while safeguarding customer data confidentiality.


Reporting:


	
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly performance reports tracking sales activities, deposit growth, and loan portfolio performance.
	


Qualifications & Requirements
Educational Qualification:


	Degree: Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Banking & Finance, Business Administration, Economics, Marketing, Accounting, or a related discipline.
	Certifications: Professional certifications in banking or finance are an added advantage.


Experience:


	1–3 years of relevant experience in banking, microfinance, sales, or relationship management.
	Direct experience in deposit mobilization and credit marketing is highly desirable.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong sales, marketing, and negotiation abilities.
	Solid relationship management and customer service orientation.
	Basic knowledge of credit analysis and financial assessments.
	Proven ability to work under pressure and achieve set targets.
	Strong understanding of microfinance/banking products and services.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.

	Professional certification in Project Management (PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent) will be an added advantage.
	Proficient use of Microsoft office and project management software.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV, Cover Letter, Portfolio of completed or ongoing projects to: [email protected] using "Application for Project Manager" as the subject of the mail.

Human Resources Manager Job at Sinoma NJZC International Construction




        
    

        
        Sinoma NJZC International Construction is a major international engineering and construction firm operating in Nigeria and West Africa. It operates as a subsidiary of Sinoma International Engineering Co., Ltd., a massive Chinese state-owned enterprise under the China National Building Material Group (CNBM).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Human Resources Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Requirements
     

            
    




	Candidates should possess relevant educational qualifications
	Previous work experience in a similar role is an advantage.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work effectively in a team and under pressure.
	Commitment to professionalism, safety, and organizational standards.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
